title: ApsBase
description: Base schema for the Agent Policy Specification v0.1.0. All other APS schemas extend this schema. Defines shared types used across the specification.

PolicyType.description: The policy language/engine type. Determines which evaluation engine is used and which transports are valid.

Transport.description: The execution transport for loading and evaluating the policy. Valid transports depend on the policy type.

FileSource.description: Loads a policy from a local file path.

path.description: Relative or absolute path to the policy file on disk.

HttpSource.description: Delegates policy evaluation to a remote HTTP endpoint.

url.description: The URL of the remote policy evaluator.

headers.description: Optional HTTP headers to include in evaluation requests (e.g. Authorization).

timeout_ms.description: Request timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to implementation-defined value.

WasmSource.description: Loads a compiled WebAssembly policy bundle from the local filesystem.

path.description: Path to the compiled .wasm policy bundle.

StdioSource.description: Spawns a subprocess and communicates over stdin/stdout (MCP-style).

args.description: Optional arguments passed to the subprocess.

env.description: Optional environment variables to set for the subprocess.

RuntimeSource.description: In-process handler registered at runtime. No transport layer applies.

handler.description: The registered handler name or class identifier for the in-process evaluator.

Metadata.description: Common metadata attached to every APS context object.

agent_id.description: Unique identifier for the agent that owns this session.

session_id.description: Unique identifier for the current session.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the interception occurred.

AssistantMessage.description: A message produced by the LLM (role must be 'assistant').

content.description: The text content of the assistant message.

Message.description: A single message in a conversation.

role.description: The role of the message author.

content.description: The text content of the message.
